commit.message:
feat: multi-select existing worktrees when attaching lanes (#140)

* feat: multi-select existing worktrees when attaching lanes

Add a new dialog that auto-discovers unregistered git worktrees and lets
users select multiple to attach as lanes at once, replacing the manual
one-at-a-time path entry. The original manual form is preserved as a
fallback for custom paths.

- Add listUnregisteredWorktrees() to laneService (parses git worktree list --porcelain)
- Wire through IPC, preload bridge, and type declarations
- New MultiAttachWorktreeDialog with checkbox list, select all, progress tracking
- Existing AttachLaneDialog kept accessible via "Use manual form" fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: indeterminate checkbox state and pre-select failed worktrees on retry

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/arul28/ADE/sessions/f9de320e-129e-4deb-8190-5f69f9627eaa

Co-authored-by: arul28 <31745423+arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments and move adopt-to-.ade option into import dialog

Rename UnregisteredWorktree → UnregisteredLaneCandidate for lane-aligned
terminology across all IPC/preload/renderer contracts. Add useEffect cleanup
flag to prevent stale state updates on unmounted dialog. Fix loop variable
null-safety in batch attach handler.

Move the "Move to .ade/worktrees" option from the lane tab hint bar into the
multi-attach worktree dialog as a red warning-gated checkbox (off by default)
with a secondary confirmation modal explaining destructive consequences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: stream full logs during setup instead of just status

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: separate boot/setup timeouts, setup gets 20 min for pip install

The pip install of lerobot from source takes ~10-15 min. Previously
boot + setup shared a single 600s timeout, so setup only got ~400s
after a slow boot. Now boot gets 600s and setup gets its own 1200s.
Also shows last log line during setup for progress visibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: log streaming reads from log file, fix sentinel, remove unused const

- _stream_logs now uses vast.execute() to tail /workspace/train.log
  instead of vast.logs() which only shows container stdout
- Training command writes TRAINING COMPLETE sentinel to the log file
- Remove unused TRAINING_STEPS constant (only used in train.py)
- Improve HF_TOKEN error message to mention hf auth login

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Added section-seat: vertical timeline view from leadership (NEW)
- Hardened section-philosophy: sharper before/after statements
- Repositioned section-system before section-plan, reduced to 4 cards
- Reframed section-plan as '90-Day Takeover' with parallel audit language
- Updated section-seo: new eyebrow/headline, LSA gap identified
- Replaced section-financial with binary With/Without System comparison
- Updated section-footprint text only (dark theme/D3/KPI unchanged)

- Updated nav dots to exactly 15 matching new section IDs
- Added CSS for breaks, inaction, seat, and financial binary layouts

commit.message:
Update docs/ai-optimizer-implementation-plan.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: later-life-planner
repository.description: UK Later-Life Planning MVP — aspiration-first financial planning for ages 50–75

commit.message:
docs: address review findings in ai-optimizer-implementation-plan

- Finding 1 (goal contract too narrow): replace Partial<WaterfallConfig> in
  goal-orchestrate with OptimizerPolicyOverride — a richer type expressing
  constraint thresholds (careReserveTarget, bequestTarget, minAnnualIncome,
  inflationAdjustSpending). Phase 6 is now explicitly scoped to evolve beyond
  the waterfall ordering family.

- Finding 2 (trust boundary broken): POST /api/optimizer-explain now accepts
  { planId } only and recomputes optimizer server-side from canonical Cosmos
  plan state. Client-supplied optimization results are explicitly prohibited.

- Finding 3 (taxRuleVersions too weak): replaced Record<string,string> with
  RuleProvenance[] — captures rule_id, version, tax_year_requested,
  tax_year_used, jurisdiction, and is_fallback per rule. MCP citation hydration
  now uses the full provenance entry.

- Finding 4 (HmrcChunk missing citation fields): added source_url,
  applicable_tax_year, and jurisdiction to HmrcChunk. retrieveHmrcChunks
  now requires taxYear and jurisdiction params and filters retrieval to
  prevent cross-year leakage. Phase 4 acceptance criterion updated to
  require source_url in response and make cross-year leakage a test failure.

- Finding 5 (Phase 1 acceptance too weak): golden fixture tests now required
  covering per-year totalTax, feasibility, asset balances, depletion year,
  terminalAssets (±£1), and strategy sequence. Fixtures stored in
  tests/financialEngine/fixtures/ as JSON.

Also: added terminalAssets field to OptimizationResult.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: later-life-planner
repository.description: UK Later-Life Planning MVP — aspiration-first financial planning for ages 50–75

commit.message:
fix(brand): align website with canonical brand palette, deploy font, replace favicon (#36)

* fix(brand): align website with canonical brand palette, deploy Bloom Play font, replace favicon

- Update CSS custom properties to canonical brand colors (#4CD2CF teal, #17181F near-black, #2B2F86 indigo)
- Replace all hardcoded Tailwind teal rgba(20,184,166) with brand teal rgba(76,210,207)
- Replace all hardcoded Tailwind stone rgba(12,10,9) with brand near-black rgba(23,24,31)
- Deploy BloomPlay-Bold.woff2 with @font-face and font-display: swap
- Apply Bloom Play Bold to h1 headings
- Replace old bloom/notch favicon.svg with zen-owl icon-192.png in all HTML pages
- Update theme-color meta to #17181F in all HTML pages and site.webmanifest
- Update validate_site.py to require font instead of old favicon
- Add font preload for LCP optimization on index.html
- Remove stale dash/ redirect directory

commit.message:
docs: add AI-enabled optimizer implementation plan

7-phase delivery plan for the full AI-enabled drawdown optimizer,
based on the reconciled architecture in optimizer-architecture-reconciled.md.

Phases:
  1. Optimizer core port — scripts/combined-strategy.ts → src/financialEngine/
  2. Optimizer UI panel — OptimizerPanel.tsx in Step 4 dashboard
  3. Explanation API route — POST /api/optimizer-explain, provider-agnostic LLM
  4. Cosmos RAG retrieval — src/lib/hmrcRag.ts, DiskANN vector search
  5. Audit trace route — GET /api/tax-trace (unblocked, any time)
  6. Goal orchestration — GoalRegistry types + LLM config mapping
  7. Scotland jurisdiction — taxJurisdiction in PlannerState + 6-band snapshot

Each phase includes concrete file paths, type signatures, implementation
notes, and acceptance criteria. RAG MVP uses Cosmos DB DiskANN; Azure AI
Search S1 is the documented upgrade path. LLM provider is abstracted in
src/lib/llm.ts (Anthropic MVP, Azure OpenAI gpt-4.1-mini for production).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
std/net/http: expose all response headers via headers() accessor (#747)

Add hew_http_response_headers() FFI function that returns a
Vec<(String, String)> containing every captured response header as
(name, value) pairs. The vec uses ElemKind::Plain with elem_size=16
(two *mut c_char pointers per element); Hew's compiled destructor
frees the string fields when the Vec goes out of scope.

Hew-facing surface additions:
- ResponseMethods trait: fn headers(resp: Response) -> Vec<(String, String)>
- impl ResponseMethods: delegates to hew_http_response_headers(resp)
- extern "C" block: binds hew_http_response_headers

Rust implementation:
- HewStringPair #[repr(C)] struct for the element layout
- hew_http_response_headers() builds the vec from the already-captured
  headers field on HewHttpResponse; returns empty vec (never null) when
  resp is null or headers is null (fail-closed, no silent fallbacks)

Unit tests added (4):
- null resp returns non-null empty vec
- resp with null headers field returns empty vec
- two-pair roundtrip verifies order, names, and values
- single-pair roundtrip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

hew doc: fail closed on parse errors, add e2e contract tests (#746)

- Parse errors in hew doc now set had_errors=true and skip the
  file (instead of emitting a warning and continuing). This aligns
  parse-error handling with the existing fail-closed contract for
  file I/O errors.
- Each parse error is printed as 'Error: <file>: <message>' to stderr.
- Add hew-cli/tests/doc_e2e.rs with three integration tests:
    valid_file_exits_zero          - well-formed .hew exits 0
    parse_error_file_exits_nonzero - broken syntax exits nonzero
    nonexistent_file_exits_nonzero - missing path exits nonzero

commit.message:
docs(fmt): clarify multi-file --check batch behavior and add tests (#745)

- README: expand --check section to show multi-file usage example
  and explicitly document the batch semantics: per-file stderr
  message for each unformatted file, aggregate exit 1 if any file
  needs formatting, no final summary count line emitted.
- tests: add three focused e2e tests for multi-file --check:
  - all files formatted → exit 0, silent
  - some files unformatted → per-file messages, no good-file noise,
    no summary count, exit 1
  - all files unformatted → all reported, no summary count, exit 1

No formatter semantics changed; this is a reality-alignment slice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: show actual command error message instead of exit status

OsExecutor.Run now captures stderr from the subprocess. On failure,
it extracts the last non-empty line (the most actionable message for
tools like git) and returns it as the error. Falls back to the original
exit error when stderr is empty.

Before: ✗ test-repo-2    — exit status 128
After:  ✗ test-repo-2    — fatal: Remote branch lol1 not found in upstream origin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(http): fix hew_http_post_string rustdoc — null only on transport error (#742)

The previous doc comment stated 'Returns null on error or non-2xx status.'
That was wrong: the implementation only returns null when status_code < 0
(i.e. a transport/network-level failure).  Non-2xx HTTP responses (4xx, 5xx)
still yield a valid malloc'd body string.

Corrected the comment to match the actual behaviour and added a pointer to
hew_http_post for callers that need to inspect the status code directly.

No behaviour changed; docs only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
build: add narrow test-parser/types/cli make aliases and route dispatcher (#743)

Add three narrow Makefile test targets:
  - test-parser  → cargo test -p hew-parser -p hew-lexer
  - test-types   → cargo test -p hew-types -p hew-parser -p hew-lexer
  - test-cli     → cargo test -p hew-cli -p adze-cli

Update ci-preflight-dispatcher.sh parser/types/cli lanes to call the
new make targets instead of bare cargo commands, so all preflight
routing goes through named make aliases.

Add .PHONY entries and header comments for the new targets.
Document the new narrow targets in CONTRIBUTING.md.

commit.message:
codegen: deduplicate net.Connection timeout-method dispatch in MLIRGenExpr (#741)

Both the typed-handle path and the i32-typed-handle path in
generateHandleMethodCall repeated the same two-branch mapping:

  set_read_timeout  → hew_tcp_set_read_timeout
  set_write_timeout → hew_tcp_set_write_timeout

Extract a small lambda (dispatchConnectionTimeoutMethod) immediately after
the existing rtCall lambda and call it from both sites. Behaviour is
identical; the diff is purely mechanical.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(cli): stabilize eval timeout fixtures (#739)

Keep the eval timeout-only checks CPU-bound so they only exercise the timeout and exit-code path. Large stdout/stderr drain coverage already lives in dedicated eval tests, so the timeout fixtures no longer need to spam stdout and couple themselves to pipe-drain scheduling on macOS arm64.
